definition: Long-duration nominal bonds are the worst-performing asset class under fiscal dominance, returning -25% to -65% real cumulative in every historical episode without exception. Gold returns +150-400% cumulative real in every episode with free pricing. This is the most robust cross-asset return pattern across all analogues.
evidence: Cross-analogue evidence showing consistent underperformance in every fiscal dominance episode
30Y-SPX correlation at +0.05 to +0.15 means long Treasuries compound losses during equity drawdowns
Gold at $3,000+ with $400-600/oz residual above four-factor model
Central bank gold buying at 1,000+ tonnes/year
Pair_0 debate: the single highest-conviction combined finding
open_questions: Is the gold residual sustainable or does it represent speculative overshoot?
What duration exposure if any retains hedging value under partial fiscal dominance?
Does the cross-asset return pattern hold when adjusting for the unprecedented starting conditions?
